intro.lead: A heavy tape for AI today:
intro.text: a leverage unwind out of Asia hit memory and IP names hard — MU, ARM, MRVL, ASML all down sharply while SPY barely moved.
hyperscaler capex is still up strongly YoY and compute demand signals (GPU $/hr) remain firm.
the barometer reads Strong but near the lower edge — we read today as deleveraging, not a thesis break.

health.label: Strong
health.commentary: Long-term demand structure is intact (capex, compute pricing), but breadth is weak today with hyperscalers below their 50D SMA. The soft spot is the software/application layer.

positioning: Flows suggest fast-money deleveraging in high-beta semis rather than long-term reallocation; quality large-caps saw smaller drawdowns.
earnings_note: Capex momentum across the four hyperscalers remains the strongest structural support for the AI compute chain.

thesis.overall: Portfolio is concentrated in the parts of the chain that fell hardest today, but the multi-quarter demand thesis is unbroken. No verdict changes purely on a one-day leverage move.

trade_ideas.title: MU / ARM relative value
trade_ideas.rationale: Memory cycle vs rich IP multiple — favor MU over ARM on a 1-3 month horizon if the unwind stabilizes.

trade_ideas.title: Hedge the app layer
trade_ideas.rationale: Software/application names below 50D; a light index hedge (IGV puts) covers the weakest layer.

self_audit: Health score is weighted from 7 components; weights are fixed and may not suit every regime.
One-day moves dominate momentum/breadth inputs — interpret near band edges cautiously.
Capex YoY is annual-statement based and lags intra-year shifts.

glossary.d: Stacked memory used next to AI accelerators for very high data throughput.
Average closing price over the last 50 trading days — a common trend gauge.
Capital expenditure — money companies spend on long-lived assets like data centers.
A trade comparing two related assets rather than betting on market direction.
